violations.text: Menu is lacking an allergen statement. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (slicer) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Slicer is to be cleaned and sanitized before use. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/04/2025

French toast and pancake batters observed held at 50-58 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Disposed. Discussed how to properly use ice for cold holding, with ice up to the level of food on all sides. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/04/2025

Chlorine sanitizer solution was observed in excess of 200 parts per million (ppm). A chlorine sanitizer solution applied to food contact surfaces must be between 50-100 ppm. PIC instructed to check sanitizer daily. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/04/2025
The kitchen floor is in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 03/04/2026
Menu was observed lacking an allergy statement. New Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2025

At time of inspection, there was no one in the establishment with an active food safety certification. A full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety must be present at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. New Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2025

Raw animal foods were not stored properly to prevent cross-contamination. Raw eggs were observed stored above raw seafood. PIC rearranged cooler units. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (basement slicer and ice machines) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. PIC took items out of service to be washed/sanitized - ice disposed of. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2025

Per interview of staff, slicers are only washed, rinsed, and sanitized at the end of the day. The surfaces of equipment that contacts time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods such as meats and cheeses must be washed, rinsed, and sanitized at least every four hours. New Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2025

Various items were observed held between 44-49 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC placed items in another unit for active cooling. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2025

The door to the outside, located in the front, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 09/04/2025

The ice scoop (top of the ice machine) AND knives (between table and prep unit) are stored on an unclean surface between uses. During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. PIC removed items to wash/sanitize. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2025

Bowls are being used as scoops in containers of dry ingredients. Utensils used to dispense non-time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods such as sugar and flour must be stored in the food item with their handles above the top of the food within the container. PIC removed bowls. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2025
Cold drawer and prep unit door gaskets were observed in poor repair. PIC stated gaskets have been ordered. New Violation. Correct By: 09/04/2025

The floors, walls, vents throughout the facility have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (basement slicer) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Slicer cleaned and sanitized during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/22/2024

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher was 25 parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. Service called during time of inspection. Establishment must sanitize dishes at 3 bay sink until dishwasher is properly adjusted. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/22/2024

Shellstock tags are not maintained for 90 days. Shellstock tags or labels are required to be maintained for 90 calendar days from the date the container is emptied and the date when the last shellfish was used from the bag must be noted on the shellfish tag. New Violation. Correct By: 01/08/2024

Raw animal foods (such as pooled eggs) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (such as romaine and cut fruit) in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/08/2024

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher was 0 parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. Establishment must sanitize dishes at 3 bay sink until dishmachine is properly adjusted. New Violation. Correct By: 01/08/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (kitchen slicer, basement slicer and ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Ice machine was taken out of service during time of inspection. Ice machine cannot be used until equipment is washed, rinsed and sanitized. Slicers were cleaned during time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 01/08/2024

In use knives were observed stored in between prep unit lids and prep tables. During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. New Violation. Correct By: 01/08/2024

The cutting boards on the cookline are very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. New Violation. Correct By: 01/22/2024
The door to the toilet rooms are not self-closing. New Violation. Correct By: 01/22/2024
